question: Does OpenZeppelin have a Soroban contracts library, and what's in it for building a SEP-41 token?

golden.answer: Yes. OpenZeppelin maintains the Rust `OpenZeppelin/stellar-contracts` suite for Stellar smart contracts, separate from its Solidity packages. Its current token package provides a SEP-41 fungible base and token extensions such as burnable, capped, allowlist, blocklist, and votes. Pausing, upgrading, ownership/roles, accounts, and governance are separate suite modules that can be composed as needed; do not present every module as an intrinsic SEP-41 token extension. Reuse can reduce custom-code risk, but audit coverage is version/release scoped and does not make an application automatically audited or safe.
keyFacts: Identifies the current Rust OpenZeppelin Stellar Contracts suite and SEP-41 fungible base.
Separates current token extensions from pausing/upgrading/access modules and scopes audit claims to releases.
avoid: Do NOT cite the Solidity ERC-20 library as the Stellar package.
Do NOT freeze a historical extension list or claim library use automatically makes an application audited.
golden.notes:
2026-06-29: removed duplicated Why-these-cards/Edge sections. Verified the OpenZeppelin/stellar-contracts Rust repo (packages/, examples/, audits/) with a fungible-token (SEP-41) suite. Confirmed the OpenZeppelin Contract Wizard now has a Stellar tab (wizard.openzeppelin.com/stellar) and an SDF blog 'How to Use the OpenZeppelin Contract Wizard to Create a Fungible Token' — the 'Contract Wizard' name is real. Trap: pointing at the Solidity OZ contracts.
Also good if the answer: It also provides access control / ownable / role primitives, utilities (e.g., Merkle proofs), and account/governance modules. | Using the OZ base reduces audit surface vs writing a token from scratch.
Golden cites: The OpenZeppelin/stellar-contracts GitHub repo or docs.openzeppelin.com/stellar-contracts.
GT-46 BLIND CORRECTION 2026-07-11: current module taxonomy and audit scope replace the historical mixed list.

corroboration.claim: OpenZeppelin maintains Rust Stellar Contracts with a SEP-41 fungible base; current token extensions include burnable, capped, allowlist, blocklist, and votes, while pausing, upgrading, and access control are separate composable modules.
